tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated serum creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, acidic pH), suggesting significant renal dysfunction. Elevated ALT indicates possible hepatic involvement. Presence of glucose and ketones in urine may indicate poor glycemic control or metabolic stress. Low serum protein and globulin further support possible renal loss or liver dysfunction. Prompt nephrology and further metabolic evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus

text.div: Provider Organization: Azienda Sanitaria Locale n° 7 Musei
